text: Here, we present a highly efficient photoelectrochemical epoxidation catalyst system for diverse alkenes, employing Br⁻/BrO⁻ as redox mediator on an α-Fe₂O₃ photoanode and water as an oxygen source. The Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediator system affords a high selectivity (selectivities > 99%) and excellent faradaic efficiencies (up to 82 ± 4 %) that far exceed all previous reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical studies. By identification of a Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediated mechanism with non-radical oxygen atom transfer (OAT) to oxidize Br⁻ to BrO⁻ on the photoanode and subsequent selective transfer of O from BrO⁻ to the unsaturated bond of alkenes, this Br⁻/BrO⁻ mediated photoelectrochemical system provides an environmentally friendly pathway to simultaneously produce valuable-oxidized products, such as desired chemical products of high commercial value, like various type of epoxides, along with green hydrogen, as a renewable energy carrier.
